title: Bulbophyllum macranthum
intro: Bulbophyllum macranthum is a species of orchid, commonly known as the large-flowered Bulbophyllum, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum macranthum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum macranthum is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Bulbophyllum. This genus is part of the subtribe Bulbophyllinae, which is a subgroup of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The species is further categorized as being native to tropical regions of the Old World, particularly in Southeast Asia. Bulbophyllum macranthum is a type of epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants, such as trees, without harming them. It is a member of the diverse and widespread orchid family, which consists of over 30,000 known species worldwide.
